show.title: Trainspotting
show.headline: Danny Boyle's hyper-kinetic heroin culture classic

event.title: <show-title>Trainspotting</show-title> <event-type-title>30th Anniversary</event-type-title>
event.headline: Choose the big screen. Choose a brand-new 4K restoration.
event.description:
Funny, disturbing, tragic, and deeply addictive, TRAINSPOTTING, directed by Danny Boyle and written by John Hodge, is based on the seminal best-selling novel of the same name by Irvine Welsh. Set in gritty, late 1980s Edinburgh, Scotland, it’s the story of smart, funny, sickly, heroin addict Mark Renton (Ewan McGregor) and his so-called friends – a bunch of losers, liars, psychos, thieves, and junkies. Hilarious but harrowing, the film charts the disintegration of their friendship as they proceed, seemingly inevitably, towards self-destruction. Mark alone has the insight and opportunity to escape his fate, but does he really want to “choose life”?

Screening a new 4K restoration in honor of the film's 30th anniversary

show.title: But I'm a Cheerleader
show.headline: A comedy of sexual disorientation

event.title: <show-title>But I'm a Cheerleader</show-title> <event-type-title>Movie Party</event-type-title>
event.headline: Conversion camp has never been this fun
event.description: Grab your pom-poms and report to Harmony, because we’re throwing a live-hosted movie party for BUT I’M A CHEERLEADER. You’ll be decked out with themed props, including an orange and white pom-pom, a blue necktie, a hair clip, a Pride flag, and a heart carabiner as you follow Megan’s journey from suburban confusion to “I kissed a girl and I liked it” realisation. This is one movie party where there’s no reason to learn to be normal.

show.title: Funeral Parade of Roses
show.headline: An electrifying journey into the nether regions of late-‘60s Tokyo underground

event.title: <show-title>Funeral Parade of Roses</show-title>
event.headline: Matsumoto's 1969 transposition of Greek tragedy to underground queer Tokyo
event.description:
“I am a wound and a sword, a victim, and an executioner.”

In the 1960s, global cinema was in the midst of a new wave of filmmaking. Goddard, Bunuel, and Truffaut were changing the French film landscape; Forman and Chytilova were making waves in Czechoslovakia; and Cassavetes was bringing startling realism to the New Hollywood movement. In Japan, the film world was expanding massively with fresh opportunities from studios and boundless creativity from a new generation of filmmakers. While many films from this era are filled with subtextual nods to homosexuality and queer-coded characters, few engaged in the overt representation of queer culture like Toshio Matsumoto’s FUNERAL PARADE OF ROSES. With this project, Matsumoto employed elements of the angura “little theater” movement, combined with avant-garde filmmaking techniques, to develop a new methodology he coined “neo-documentarism.” The result is something transcendent, metatextual, and otherworldly, unlike any other film of its time. Casting with largely non-professional actors, Matsumoto found his lead performer in a teenage gay bar employee named Shinnosuke Ikehata, who would adopt the stage name Peter due to their androgenous appearance being likened to Peter Pan. The film loosely follows the plot of the Greek tragedy Oedipus Rex, with Peter playing the role of Eddie, a young transgender host club employee who rises in the ranks at the underground establishment while running afoul of aging madame Leda and engaging in a sexual relationship with Gonda, the bar’s owner. Honestly, it’s kinda giving SHOWGIRLS, but Japanese and far weirder and more confusing – and that’s a high bar. Strange and singular, FUNERAL PARADE OF ROSES is a quintessential film in the history of queerness on screen, and we can’t wait to watch it with you as part of Queer Film Theory 101.

show.title: The Handmaiden
show.headline: A sublime and subversive queer thriller from Park Chan-wook

event.title: <show-title>The Handmaiden</show-title> <event-type-title>Book Club Screening</event-type-title>
event.headline: Free sticker and bookmark. Ben Wa balls not included.
event.description: For readers who like their adaptations a little dangerous, this one doesn’t play nice. At this special Book Club screening of Park Chan-wook’s (NO OTHER CHOICE, OLDBOY) THE HANDMAIDEN, you’ll get a free bookmark passport with a custom sticker to track your novel-to-screen journey. Whether you’ve been collecting since your first stop or this is your initiation, join us and see how Sarah Waters’ novel Fingersmith transforms under the unique vision of director Park. Shifting loyalties, hidden identities, and carefully laid schemes unfold between Lady Hideko and her handmaiden Sook-hee. You’ll never hear the jingling of bells the same way again.

eventType.title: Book Club Screening
eventType.headline: Collect custom stickers as you enjoy your favorite movie adaptations
eventType.description: Celebrate your favorite book-turned-movie with the Alamo Drafthouse Movie Book Club. Get your passport at the show, then fill it with custom-themed stickers at each Book Club screening. Join us in the bar after the film to connect with local bookstores, libraries, and other book lovers. Keep reading and keep watching!

show.title: Cape Fear (1991)
show.headline: Martin Scorsese's southern gothic nightmare

event.title: <show-title>Cape Fear (1991)</show-title> <event-type-title>Free Victory Screening</event-type-title>
event.headline: Celebrate the 35th anniversary of this Scorsese-De Niro classic for free
event.description:
Before the Cape Fear Apple TV series debuts on June 5, catch this tense 1991 thriller starring Robert De Niro and Nick Nolte on the big screen for free. Directed by Martin Scorsese, CAPE FEAR is itself a remake of the 1962 noir starring Robert Mitchum and Gregory Peck, but Marty cranks up the intensity (and the tattoos) for the '90s. With memorable supporting performances by Jessica Lange and Juliette Lewis, a killer score, and an unforgettable scene in a movie theater, CAPE FEAR is worth revisiting in theaters. Just leave your cigars at home.

Pair the movie with our Cape-inspired menu, featuring Cady's Contraband Popcorn, Trial By Fire Wings, the Storm Warning cocktail, and Cape Fear Punch.

Reserve your spot today by purchasing a small, fully refundable food and beverage voucher redeemable for food and non-alcoholic drinks at the show. Then be sure to tune in June 5 on Apple TV for the Martin Scorsese and Steven Spielberg-produced series starring Javier Bardem, Amy Adams, and Patrick Wilson.

eventType.title: Free Victory Screening
eventType.headline: Our way of saying thank you to our Victory Members
eventType.description: Calling all Victory Members! We’re saying thanks with a free screening. Just buy a fully refundable food & beverage voucher to reserve your seat, and then use it toward food and drink at the show.

titleCardImage.altText: Event Category Web Hero Free Victory Screening 1920x1080

show.title: "Spider-Noir"
show.headline: A down-on-his-luck private investigator is the city's one and only superhero

event.title: <show-title>"Spider-Noir"</show-title> <event-type-title>w/Live Q&A</event-type-title>
event.headline: See "Spider-Noir" early and free with a live Q&A
event.description:
Be among the first to experience “Spider-Noir” with our exclusive Victory screening of the first two episodes of the Prime Video series, starring Nicolas Cage as a hardboiled PI with a secret. Set in 1930s New York, the series blends noir grit with superhero stakes.

Lamorne Morris and Jack Huston, who play Robbie Robertson and Flint Marko/Sandman, respectively, in the series, will appear at Alamo Drafthouse’s downtown Los Angeles location for a post-screening Q&A. Enjoy our limited-edition diner-inspired menu at the event, then catch the full series May 27 on Prime Video. Reserve your spot with a refundable food and beverage voucher, redeemable at the show.

contentBlocks.paragraphs: Be among the first to experience “Spider-Noir” with our exclusive Victory screening of the first two episodes of the Prime Video series, starring Nicolas Cage as a hardboiled PI with a secret. Set in 1930s New York, the series blends noir grit with superhero stakes. Lamorne Morris, who plays Robbie Robertson in the series, will appear at the Alamo Drafthouse Downtown Los Angeles event for a post-screening Q&A. Enjoy our limited-edition diner-inspired menu at the event, then catch the full series May 27 on Prime Video. Reserve your spot with a refundable food and beverage voucher, redeemable at the show.

imageAsset.title: Spider Noir Menu Web Deluxe Showpage 1920x1080

image.altText: Spider Noir Menu Web Deluxe Showpage 1920x1080

contentBlocks.paragraphs: The noir experience doesn’t stop at the screen. Every Web of Shadows screening features a themed menu inspired by 1930s diner fare and the stark contrasts of black-and-white cinema. Dig into Popcorn Noir, a darkly seasoned twist on a classic, or The City Dog, a no-nonsense all-beef frankfurter built for late nights and bad decisions. Pair it with a Black Manhattan or a brooding Dark & Stormy, cocktails as moody and complex as the characters on screen.

event.title: <show-title>Saccharine</show-title><event-type-title> with Live Q&A</event-type-title>
event.headline: Actor     Midori Francis Live in Person
event.description:
In-person Q&A with actor Midori Francis following the screening

Hana, a lovelorn medical student, becomes terrorized by a sinister force after taking part in an obscure weight-loss craze: eating human ashes.

Warning: Please be advised that SACCHARINE contains scenarios that some viewers might find distressing. In particular, it may be challenging for people who live with or have experienced eating disorders and negative body image.

show.title: I Love Boosters
show.headline: Steal this movie

event.title: <show-title>I Love Boosters</show-title><event-type-title> with Livestream Q&A</event-type-title>
event.headline: See it early with a Boots livestream Q&A
event.description: Get in early on I LOVE BOOSTERS, where style is currency and the line between survival and self-expression gets slippery. Stick around after the screening for a livestream Q&A with director Boots Riley as he unpacks the film’s high-fashion heists and the real cost of looking this good. Plus, you can donate your gently used and cleaned clothing to people in need. Just look for the special I LOVE BOOSTERS clothing donation boxes in the theater. In addition, you can enjoy an exclusive menu featuring the Boosted Chicken Club, the Fashion Forward Filanthropy Margarita, and the cherry-flavored Velvet Gang Rustler Shake topped with whipped cream.

event.description: Immortality has never looked this messy. Join a live-hosted movie party for DEATH BECOMES HER, complete with props, keepsakes, and a crowd ready to celebrate every twisted turn of this iconic dark comedy. You’ll get a frenemies bracelet to share with your best friend, plus a light-up wand to create your own special effects in the theater as the rivalry between Madeline and Helen spirals out of control.

show.title: Showgirls
show.headline: Paul Verhoeven's high glam & camp satire

event.title: <show-title>Showgirls</show-title> <event-type-title>Movie Party</event-type-title>
event.headline: Thrust it at this glitter-soaked movie party
event.description: It’s Vegas excess at full tilt with a live-hosted movie party where every turn is bigger, brasher, and more quotable than the last. Pay homage to the choreography of Marty as the story barrels through topless ambition, unforgettable stage numbers, vigorous pool sex, and that iconic delivery of “It’s Ver-sayce.” You’ll get a bag of pearls, nail stickers, glitter pasties, and a spray bottle for that showgirl sheen. As Cristal Connors says, “If someone gets in your way, step on ‘em.”

titleCardImage.altText: Event Category Web Hero Special Event 1920x1080

show.title: Grey Gardens (1975)
show.headline: The Maysles Brothers' cult classic documentary in a 2K restoration

event.title: <show-title>Grey Gardens (1975)</show-title>
event.headline: The 50th Anniversary of this iconic documentary classic.
event.description: Meet Big and Little Edie Beale: mother and daughter, high-society dropouts and reclusive cousins of Jackie Onassis. The two manage to thrive together amid the decay and disorder of their East Hampton, NY mansion, making for an eerily ramshackle echo of the American Camelot. An impossibly intimate portrait, this 1976 documentary by Albert and David Maysles, codirected by Ellen Hovde and Muffie Meyer, quickly became a cult classic and established Little Edie as a fashion icon and philosopher queen.

show.title: Godzilla vs. Hedorah

show.title: Frances Ha
show.headline: Greta Gerwig follows her dwindling dreams while navigating NYC

event.title: <show-title>Frances Ha</show-title>
event.headline: Gerwig dances through her early 20s in the best coming of age film of the 2010s
event.description:
2012 was the age of Millennial Ennui – Lena Dunham’s Girls was a think piece darling, you couldn’t go anywhere without hearing “Somebody That I Used to Know” by Gotye, and Greta Gerwig (with the help of future husband Noah Baumbach) was making a splash with her French New Wave-inspired critical darling FRANCES HA.

Shot entirely in black and white, FRANCES HA follows aspiring dancer Frances (Gerwig) as she tumbles awkwardly through her early 20s in New York City. She has trouble keeping up with friends, constantly feels low on cash, and career setbacks feel like monumental blows. She successfully taps into the universal feeling everyone gets as a young adult: ambitious yet aimless. With a knock-out script and performance from Gerwig, FRANCES HA is the blueprint for her future as an incredibly thoughtful coming-of-age filmmaker.

titleCardImage.altText: Event Category Web Hero Special Event 1920x1080

show.title: Persona (1966)
show.headline: Ingmar Bergman's most personal and original film

event.title: <show-title>Persona (1966)</show-title>
event.headline: Ingmar Bergman's most personal and original film
event.description:
Ingmar Bergman’s PERSONA focuses on Alma (Bibi Andersson), a nurse whose patient – popular actress Elisabet Vogler (Liv Ullmann) – has suddenly stopped speaking. After they move to a cottage, Alma begins to confide in Volger, and the line between patient and caretaker begins to muddle.

Seen by many as one of the greatest films ever made, Bergman’s most personal film is complex, twisted, sensual, and at times utterly hopeless. Andersson and Ullmann are completely engrossing in their roles, taking the viewer down a rabbit hole of psychological unease that’s not unlike a horror movie. PERSONA is beautifully shot and a brilliant web of the female psyche, but above all, it is a staggering achievement in sad girldom.

titleCardImage.altText: Event Category Web Hero Special Event 1920x1080

show.title: Chime + Serpent's Path Double Feature
show.headline: Kiyoshi Kurosawa's mid-length curio paired with a newly restored stunner

event.title: <show-title>Chime + Serpent's Path Double Feature</show-title>
event.headline: Kiyoshi Kurosawa's mid-length curio paired with a newly restored stunner
event.description:
About CHIME
2024 / 45 min.

A masterclass in escalating dread and shocking violence, CHIME reaffirms Kiyoshi Kurosawa as one of modern horror’s most innovative and unpredictable visionaries. During a class, culinary instructor Matsuoka (Mutsuo Yoshioka) witnesses the suicide of a young student (Seiichi Kohinata), driven to insanity by what he claims is a chiming sound that controls his mind. Soon, Matsuoka begins hearing it too, and descends into a mental abyss that warps his perception of reality and gives vent to his darkest impulses. Expertly blending psychological portraiture and hallucinatory mystery, Kurosawa offers a chilling depiction of madness that interrogates the very stability of our everyday existence, with the director’s patented creeping tracking shots and complex sound design fashioning an immersively terrifying and unnerving cinematic experience.

About SERPENT'S PATH
1998 / 85 min.

Straight off CURE (1997), his international breakthrough, Kiyoshi Kurosawa directed two low-budget films using the same basic premise and the same lead actor (Sho Aikawa) to completely different ends. The experiment first resulted in SERPENT'S PATH (1998, later remade in 2024), a dark gangland thriller with philosophical overtones. Obsessed with avenging his young daughter’s murder, yakuza subordinate Miyashita (Teruyuki Kagawa) recruits Nijima (Aikawa), a brilliant yet strangely detached math teacher, to help carry out a scheme to kidnap and torture the man allegedly responsible. But the plan goes awry when their target, Otsuki (Yurei Yanagi), fingers another mobster as the mastermind behind Miyashita’s tragedy. As the two partners ascend the yakuza chain of command in search of the true culprit, Miyashita and Nijima follow the cold, calculating logic of revenge, descending into a moral abyss from which they may never surface. Featuring Kurosawa’s patented long takes and his claustrophobic arrangement of space, SERPENT'S PATH is one of the legendary director’s most chilling investigations into the endless cycle of violence and the evil that lodges in every heart.

titleCardImage.altText: Event Category Web Hero Special Event 1920x1080

show.title: The Suckling
show.headline: ... all he wanted was a cuddle

event.title: <show-title>The Suckling</show-title>
event.headline: ...all he wanted was a cuddle
event.description:
THE SUCKLING has everything you love: exploding heads, severed hands, a fully articulated beastie, major problems with major appliances, and spinning dildos. The monster effects are a feat, but the cinematography and script are just as strong. There are characters you love to hate and hate to love. This film is designed to offend. It's about abortions and contains sexual assault. But like the best exploitation films, THE SUCKLING is deceptively intelligent and dances nimbly around both sides of a charged issue. It's exploitation and horror at its finest. (Annie Choi)

Note: This film includes a brief scene of a non-consensual sexual act.

DCP courtesy of Vinegar Syndrome and the American Genre Film Archive

show.title: Ticks
show.headline: Something hungry is about to hatch

event.title: <show-title>Ticks</show-title>
event.headline: Something hungry is about to hatch
event.description:
An assortment of unruly and misfit teens have all been forced to attend camp at a wildlife preserve, to commune with nature. What they don't realize is that nearby in the woods, an illicit marijuana growing operation is underway and the weed farmers have been using a pesticide which has had one very unintended side effect: turning ordinary ticks into giant, ravenous bloodsuckers...

One of the quintessential creature features of the early 90s, Tony Randel's (HELLBOUND: HELLRAISER II, AMITYVILLE II: IT'S ABOUT TIME) TICKS pays loving tribute to 1950s giant monster movies, updating the formula by adding copious amounts of slime and heavily increased bloodshed. Starring Seth Green (AUSTIN POWERS), Alfonso Ribeiro (TV's FRESH PRINCE OF BEL AIR), Rosalind Allen (TV's SEINFELD) and a very memorable appearance from Clint Howard (ICE CREAM MAN), TICKS is newly remastered in 4K. (Vinegar Syndrome)

show.title: White of the Eye
show.headline: The only difference between a hunter and a killer... is his prey

event.title: <show-title>White of the Eye</show-title>
event.headline: A cut above the rest
event.description:
If you're a wealthy, attractive woman, stay out of Arizona, because you are fair game. A twisted killer is on the loose, who tortures and dissects his beautiful victims as part of a primitive Native American ritual. All the clues lead to one man who is clearly innocent. But nothing is as simple as that.The man, a sound expert, is targeted as the prime suspect in connection to f brutal murders of local suburban housewives, all who were attacked and mutilated in their homes. As he desperately tries to prove his innocence, his wife starts to uncover startling truths. David Keith and Cathy Moriarty star in this shocking mystery-thriller co-written and directed by Donald Cammell, director of DEMON SEED and co-director of PERFORMANCE. The film also features an eclectic score by Nick Mason (Pink Floyd) and Rick Fen. Lost amongst the rubble of mid to late 80's slashers, what emerges is less your typical HALLOWEEN-knockoff but a razor sharp underlining of the the hippie dream erosion - the last fruits of Americana rotting off the vine in the Arizona sun.

DCP courtesy of Shout! Factory and the American Genre Film Archive

show.title: By Design
show.headline: A woman envies the life of a perfect chair so much that she swaps bodies with it

event.title: <show-title>By Design</show-title>
event.headline: A woman envies the life of a perfect chair so much that she swaps bodies with it
event.description: Upon seeing a gorgeous chair in a showroom, Camille (Juliette Lewis) realizes that she truly envies the life of this perfect piece of furniture. If only she could be someone’s favorite thing. When she and the chair swap forms, Camille learns that she is better liked as an inanimate object than she was as a person: her mother (Betty Buckley) finds her to be a better listener, and her best friends (Samantha Mathis and Robin Tunney) enjoy a newfound, friction-free rapport. As the chair, Camille is unable to speak or move, and winds up in the hands of Olivier (Mamoudou Athie), a minimalist bachelor who gradually becomes romantically fixated on his elegant new possession. Narrated by Melanie Griffith, this modern fable celebrates the messiness of female interiority with deadpan humor, surrealist flourishes, and interpretative dance numbers. An absurdist spin on the body-swap comedy, BY DESIGN is a bold new work from singular filmmaker Amanda Kramer.

show.title: The Devil Queen
show.headline: A lurid, fabulous dive into Brazil's criminal underworld

event.title: <show-title>The Devil Queen</show-title>
event.headline: A lurid, fabulous dive into Brazil's criminal underworld
event.description: Rio de Janeiro’s criminal underworld is run by an unexpected boss: Diaba (Milton Gonçalves), a femme queen with a taste for power and violence. When the police come after her boy toy, Diaba attempts to find a scapegoat to take the blame and a plot to dethrone Her Majesty arises! Always clad in gloriously colorful outfits and accessories inspired by Afro-Brazilian culture, Diaba runs a mob of eccentric misfits made up of drag queens, pimps, prostitutes, and queer folks of all stripes. The movie’s bold and audacious color palette and camp aesthetic evokes the early films of Pedro Almodóvar, while its stylized and over-the-top violence is giving Quentin Tarantino queer fever dream.

marketStrings.value: The personal closed captioning device displays feature film dialogue and other audio in the form of English-language text for individuals who are deaf or hard of hearing.

The descriptive audio headset provides individuals who are blind or have low vision with an English-language narration of the details of the feature presentation that can't be understood by the soundtrack alone.

Upon request at the box office, we provide assistive listening headsets with amplified audio for those individuals who are hard of hearing. For guests who prefer to use their own hearing aids, neck loops, or cochlear implants, our assistive listening headsets have a 3.5mm headphone jack to connect to those devices.

In order to ensure the highest quality experience for all guests, you may not enter a movie after it has started. We’d love to see you up to 30 minutes early. Running late? No worries, we will happily exchange your ticket for a different showtime or a raincheck to a future show.

sessionAttributes.description: For our baby day shows, we keep the lights up a bit, and the sound down a bit, so your little one never has to know they even went to the movies!
